You don't need a computer science degree to turn messy research and student data into stunning, publication-ready insights.

Join the Free 5-Day Kit

Let’s be honest. As academics, we are caught in a relentless vice grip. Between crushing teaching loads, endless administrative bureaucracy, and the non-negotiable pressure to "publish or perish," there are simply not enough hours in the day.

To make matters worse, we’re often still wrestling with clunky legacy tools like SPSS, or manually fighting with Excel formulas just to analyze a simple survey or grade book. You see the world moving toward modern data science, but you don't have the time to learn how to code from scratch. You might feel left behind, assuming this "modern data world" is reserved for tech-bro developers.

It’s not. And you don't have to get left behind.

  • ⚡ Reclaim Your Sanity (And Your Weekends) Automate the repetitive headache of student test scores and grading metrics. Learn the exact Excel workflows that turn hours of manual spreadsheet tracking into a 5-minute task.
  • 📊 Ditch the Clunky, 90s-Style Charts Stop submitting ugly legacy graphs to peer-reviewed journals. Discover how to build stunning, interactive Power BI dashboards that immediately capture the attention of high-impact journal reviewers and grant committees.
  • 🐍 Python Without the Coding Fluff Skip the heavy developer syntax. You will learn the exact 10% of Python libraries (like Pandas) that do 90% of the heavy lifting for cleaning and analyzing complex research survey data. No computer science degree required.
  • 🚀 Future-Proof Your Academic Career Establish yourself as the go-to data expert in your department. Whether you are in STEM, Social Sciences, or the Humanities, modern data literacy is the ultimate leverage for securing promotions, international research fellowships, and global grants.

The Free 5-Day Starter Kit Roadmap

Why Listen to Me?

Hello, I’m Dr. Oluwatunmise Iwayemi.

I am not an untouchable tech guru, and I didn't start out as a software programmer. I am a fellow academic holding a PhD, working right here in higher education in Nigeria, and actively applying modern data analytics to my own research and teaching.

Like you, I know exactly what it feels like to drown under a mountain of scripts to grade, lectures to prepare, and papers to write, all while wrestling with clunky legacy software that feels decades out of date.

I originally turned to modern data tools out of pure necessity—to survive the academic workload and reclaim my weekends. But in doing so, I unlocked a massive realization: these skills are the ultimate career leverage. By stripping away the developer fluff and learning how to apply Excel, Power BI, Python, and SQL directly to my work, I didn't just save my sanity; I completely future-proofed my career for global, high-value opportunities.

Addressing Your Concerns & Hesitations

Absolutely. You do not need to become a software engineer. Developers learn Python to build apps, build games, or manage servers. As academics, we only need a tiny fraction of Python to clean messy survey tables, run correlations, and plot trends. I will show you the exact 10% of code that handles 90% of your data workload—zero coding experience required.

SPSS is great for standard statistical models, but it is slow, expensive, and outputs clunky visual graphs that look like they were designed in the 90s. High-impact international journals and global grant committees increasingly look for cutting-edge visualizations and interactive dashboards (like Power BI) that can communicate your findings instantly. Learning these modern tools ensures your research matches global standards.

This training is built specifically for busy educators. It's a 5-day kit, delivered in tiny, digestible 10-minute reads. The goal isn't to add to your workload; it’s to drastically reduce it. By learning to automate student grade sheets and metrics on Day 1 and Day 2, you will save hours of manual typing every semester, giving you your weekends back.

No, data knows no discipline boundaries. Whether you analyze qualitative interview codes, linguistic data, historical records, or demographic questionnaires, the combination of Excel, Power BI, and Python allows you to find patterns and illustrate your arguments in a way that words alone cannot. It is just as relevant to a historian or sociologist as it is to an engineer.

Yes, it is 100% free. I built this kit because I got tired of seeing brilliant minds in Nigerian higher education struggle with outdated systems and overly technical tutorials. My goal is to equip you with a highly practical shortcut so you can excel in your research. If you love the starter kit, you will have opportunities to study further with me down the line, but this 5-day kit is yours to keep, completely free.
